mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales captures aggregate consumer demand momentum; higher demand drives corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: If twenty-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS increases are not positive in more than fifty percent of instances over a three-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and consumption trajectories; equity markets reprice lower as earnings expectations contract within the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive twenty-day returns in greater than fifty-five percent of observations when UNRATE has risen more than zero point three percentage points over the prior three months.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of an elevated federal funds rate and a rising unemployment rate produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because restrictive monetary policy combined with labor market weakening amplifies recession risk beyond the independent effects of either series.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive sixty-day returns in more than fifty percent of observations, or the interaction term's t-statistic falls below one point five in a five-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In the early phase of a cyclical rebound, a month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals capacity utilization recovery and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive five-day returns occur in fewer than fifty-two percent of months when INDPRO is accelerating during identified recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds four percent, restrictive monetary policy elevates short-term discount rates and credit spreads, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive five-day returns in more than fifty percent of instances when FEDFUNDS is above four percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and payroll employment confirms a virtuous cycle of income and spending; the interaction term predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month beyond the linear effects of either series.
failure_condition: The RSAFS and PAYEMS interaction term fails to achieve a Rank IC greater than zero point zero three over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, even modest increases in the unemployment rate signal impending demand contraction and profit margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: During late-cycle periods, UNRATE increases are followed by positive sixty-day returns in more than forty-five percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in industrial production signal supply-side disruptions and falling capacity utilization, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Negative twenty-day returns occur in fewer than fifty-five percent of instances following a one percent month-over-month drop in INDPRO.
